rawText:
Good morning. How are you? It's been great, hasn't it? I've been blown away by the whole thing. In fact, I'm leaving.

There have been three themes running through the conference which are relevant to what I want to talk about. One is the extraordinary evidence of human creativity in all of the presentations that we've had and in all of the people here; just the variety of it and the range of it. The second is that it's put us in a place where we have no idea what's going to happen in terms of the future. No idea how this may play out.

I have an interest in education. Actually, what I find is, everybody has an interest in education. Don't you? I find this very interesting. If you're at a dinner party, and you say you work in education—actually, you're not often at dinner parties, frankly. If you work in education, you're not asked. And you're never asked back, curiously. That's strange to me.

But if you are, and you say to somebody, you know, they say, "What do you do?" and you say you work in education, you can see the blood run from their face. They're like, "Oh my God. Why me?" "My one night out all week."

But if you ask about their education, they pin you to the wall, because it's one of those things that goes deep with people, am I right? Like religion and money and other things. So I have a big interest in education, and I think we all do. We have a huge vested interest in it, partly because it's education that's meant to take us into this future that we can't grasp.

If you think of it, children starting school this year will be retiring in 2065. Nobody has a clue, despite all the expertise that's been on parade for the past four days, what the world will look like in five years' time. And yet, we're meant to be educating them for it. So the unpredictability, I think, is extraordinary.

And the third part of this is that we've all agreed, nonetheless, on the really extraordinary capacities that children have—their capacities for innovation. I mean, Sirena last night was a marvel, wasn't she? Just seeing what she could do. And she's exceptional, but I think she's not, so to speak, exceptional in the whole of childhood. What you have there is a person of extraordinary dedication who found a talent. And my contention is, all kids have tremendous talents, and we squander them, pretty ruthlessly.

So I want to talk about education, and I want to talk about creativity. My contention is that creativity now is as important in education as literacy, and we should treat it with the same status.

sentences.original: It's been great, hasn't it? I've been blown away by the whole thing. In fact, I'm leaving.
sentences.translation: 정말 대단했죠, 그렇지 않나요? 저는 이 모든 것에 완전히 압도당했습니다. 사실, 저는 떠나려고요.

vocabulary.example: The performance blew the audience away.
I enjoyed the whole thing from start to finish.

expressions.usage: 동의를 구하는 부가의문문. 긍정문 뒤에 부정 꼬리 질문
강한 감동이나 놀라움을 비격식적으로 표현할 때
유머를 위한 과장법. "너무 좋아서 더 볼 필요가 없다"는 농담

patterns.pattern: It's been [adjective], hasn't it?
patterns.explanation: 현재완료 + 부가의문문으로 공감대를 형성하는 패턴. 강연 오프닝에서 청중과 유대감을 만듦.
examples: It's been wonderful, hasn't it?
It's been a long day, hasn't it?

drillQuestions.question: It's been great, _____ it?
I've been blown _____ by the whole thing.

sentences.notes: 부가의문문(tag question)은 영어 구어체의 핵심 장치. 긍정문 → 부정 꼬리(hasn't it), 부정문 → 긍정 꼬리. "blown away"는 바람에 날려가다의 비유에서 온 관용표현. "I'm leaving"은 청중 웃음을 유도하는 전형적 TED 농담.
pronunciationNotes: hasn't it: /ˈhæznt ɪt/ — 빠르게 "해즌릿"으로 연음, t는 거의 탈락
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 유머(humor) — 과장된 칭찬 후 "떠나겠다"는 농담으로 청중의 웃음과 호감을 동시에 확보

sentences.original: There have been three themes running through the conference which are relevant to what I want to talk about.
sentences.translation: 이 컨퍼런스를 관통하는 세 가지 주제가 있었는데, 제가 이야기하고 싶은 것과 관련이 있습니다.

grammarElements.text: which are relevant to what I want to talk about
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (which — themes를 수식)

vocabulary.example: The main theme of the book is love and loss.
This information is relevant to our discussion.
A sense of urgency runs through his writing.

expressions.usage: 주제나 아이디어가 전체에 걸쳐 이어질 때. 현재분사의 형용사적 용법
특정 주제와의 연관성을 표현할 때

patterns.pattern: There have been [number] [nouns] which are relevant to [topic]
patterns.explanation: 강연 도입부에서 다룰 주제를 미리 예고하는 구조(signposting). 청중이 논점을 따라가기 쉽게 만듦.
examples: There have been two developments which are relevant to this debate.
There have been several issues running through the discussion which I want to address.

drillQuestions.question: There have been three themes _____ through the conference.
These themes are _____ to what I want to talk about.

sentences.notes: running through는 현재분사의 후치수식으로 themes를 수식한다. "themes that are running through"를 축약한 형태. which 관계대명사 안에 다시 what 관계대명사가 내포된 복합 구조.

koreanAlignment.en: what I want to talk about

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 구조 예고(signposting) — "세 가지 주제"를 미리 밝혀 청중의 기대를 설정하고 논리적 흐름을 제시

sentences.original: One is the extraordinary evidence of human creativity in all of the presentations that we've had and in all of the people here; just the variety of it and the range of it.
sentences.translation: 하나는 우리가 본 모든 프레젠테이션과 이곳의 모든 사람들에게서 드러난 인간 창의성의 놀라운 증거입니다. 그 다양성과 범위가 놀라웠습니다.

grammarElements.text: the extraordinary evidence of human creativity
grammarElements.label: 보어

grammarElements.text: in all of the presentations that we've had
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (장소) + 관계대명사절

grammarElements.text: and in all of the people here
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (대상)

grammarElements.text: just the variety of it and the range of it
grammarElements.label: 동격 부연 (부가 설명)

vocabulary.example: She showed extraordinary talent at a young age.
The project required a lot of creativity.
There was a wide variety of food at the buffet.

expressions.phrase: the variety of it and the range of it
expressions.usage: 추상적 개념의 폭과 넓이를 강조하는 병렬 표현

patterns.pattern: One is [X]; just the [noun] of it and the [noun] of it.
patterns.explanation: 열거의 첫 번째 항목을 제시하고, 세미콜론 뒤에 감탄조로 부연하는 구어적 강조 패턴.
examples: One is the sheer amount of innovation; just the scale of it and the speed of it.
One is the quality of the work; just the depth of it and the precision of it.

drillQuestions.question: One is the _____ evidence of human creativity.
Just the variety of it and the _____ of it.

sentences.notes: "just the variety of it and the range of it"는 문법적으로 독립된 문장이 아니라, 앞 문장에 대한 감탄적 부연이다. 구어 영어에서 세미콜론 뒤에 명사구만으로 감탄을 표현하는 방식.

koreanAlignment.en: the extraordinary evidence of human creativity
in all of the presentations that we've had
and in all of the people here
just the variety of it and the range of it

pronunciationNotes: extraordinary: /ɪkˈstrɔːrdɪneri/ — "익스트로어디너리", 6음절, 강세는 두 번째 음절. extra+ordinary 합성이 아니라 독자적 리듬

sentences.original: The second is that it's put us in a place where we have no idea what's going to happen in terms of the future.
sentences.translation: 두 번째는 이것이 우리를 미래에 어떤 일이 일어날지 전혀 모르는 상황에 놓았다는 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: that it's put us in a place
grammarElements.label: that절 (보어) — 현재완료

grammarElements.text: where we have no idea what's going to happen
grammarElements.label: 관계부사절 (where) + 명사절 (what)

vocabulary.example: The crisis put us in a difficult place.
I have no idea what time it starts.

expressions.usage: don't know보다 강한 표현으로, 완전한 무지를 강조
특정 측면이나 분야를 한정할 때 사용하는 담화 표지

patterns.pattern: It's put us in a place where we have no idea [what/how/when] ...
patterns.explanation: 현재 상황의 불확실성을 표현하는 패턴. 관계부사 where + 간접의문문의 중첩.
examples: Technology has put us in a place where we have no idea what jobs will exist.
Globalization has put us in a place where we have no idea how markets will react.

drillQuestions.question: It's put us in a place _____ we have no idea.
We have no idea _____ going to happen.

sentences.notes: 이 문장은 3중 내포 구조: that절 안에 where절, where절 안에 what절. "it's put"에서 it's = it has (현재완료). "in terms of the future"는 학술적/격식적 담화 표지로, 구어에서 과용되는 표현이기도 하다.

koreanAlignment.en: that it's put us in a place

pronunciationNotes: it's put: /ɪts pʊt/ — 여기서 it's = it has. "잇스 풋", has가 's로 축약
in terms of: /ɪn tɜːrmz əv/ — "인 터엄즈 어브", 빠르게 발음하면 "인텀저브"

sentences.original: I have an interest in education. Actually, what I find is, everybody has an interest in education.
sentences.translation: 저는 교육에 관심이 있습니다. 사실, 제가 발견한 것은 모든 사람이 교육에 관심이 있다는 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: everybody has an interest in education
grammarElements.label: 주절 (보어)

vocabulary.example: She has a keen interest in science.
Actually, I changed my mind.

expressions.usage: 'be interested in'보다 격식적인 표현
자신의 관찰 결과를 소개할 때. 의사 분열문(pseudo-cleft) 구조

patterns.pattern: What I find is, [observation]
patterns.explanation: 의사 분열문(pseudo-cleft). what절이 주어 역할을 하며 관찰 결과를 강조하는 구어적 패턴.
examples: What I find is, people are more creative than they think.
What I notice is, students learn better through practice.

drillQuestions.question: _____ I find is, everybody has an interest in education.
I have an interest _____ education.

sentences.notes: "what I find is"는 의사 분열문(pseudo-cleft sentence)으로, 강조하고 싶은 내용을 뒤로 배치한다. 직설적인 "I find that everybody..."보다 구어적이고 청중의 주의를 끈다.

koreanAlignment.en: I have an interest in education
everybody has an interest in education
koreanAlignment.ko: 모든 사람이 교육에 관심이 있다는 것입니다

sentences.original: If you're at a dinner party, and you say you work in education—actually, you're not often at dinner parties, frankly.
sentences.translation: 만약 당신이 디너 파티에 있는데 교육 분야에서 일한다고 말하면—사실, 솔직히 교육 분야에서 일하면 디너 파티에 잘 초대받지 못하죠.

grammarElements.text: If you're at a dinner party
grammarElements.label: 조건절 (if)

grammarElements.text: and you say you work in education
grammarElements.label: 등위절 + 명사절 (say + that 생략)

grammarElements.text: you're not often at dinner parties
grammarElements.label: 주절 (삽입 — 자기 수정)

vocabulary.example: We hosted a dinner party for ten guests.
Frankly, I think this plan will fail.

expressions.usage: 'work in + 분야'로 직업 분야를 표현
앞 내용을 수정하거나 불편한 진실을 말할 때 완충 역할

patterns.pattern: If you're at [place], and you say [X]—actually, [self-correction]
patterns.explanation: 가정을 시작한 후 스스로 끊고 수정하는 구어적 패턴. 계획된 유머를 자연스러운 탈선처럼 연출.
examples: If you're at a party and you mention your job—actually, people don't usually ask about jobs at parties.
If you tell someone you're a writer—actually, most people don't know what that means.

drillQuestions.question: If you're _____ a dinner party, you might say you work in education.
You're not often at dinner parties, _____.

sentences.notes: 이 문장은 Robinson의 특유의 자기 수정(self-correction) 유머 기법을 보여준다. 가정을 세우다가 스스로 끊고 "사실 그 가정 자체가 성립하지 않는다"며 웃음을 유도. 대시(—)가 구어적 중단을 나타낸다.

koreanAlignment.en: If you're at a dinner party
and you say you work in education
you're not often at dinner parties

pronunciationNotes: dinner party: /ˈdɪnər ˈpɑːrti/ — "디너 파아티", 미국식에서 r 발음 주의
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 자기 수정(self-interruption) — 자신의 말을 스스로 끊고 수정하며 자연스러운 유머를 창출하는 Robinson 특유의 기법

sentences.original: If you work in education, you're not asked. And you're never asked back, curiously. That's strange to me.
sentences.translation: 교육 분야에서 일하면 초대받지 못합니다. 그리고 이상하게도, 한 번도 다시 초대받지 못하죠. 저에게는 이상한 일입니다.

vocabulary.example: They never asked us back after that party.
Curiously, no one noticed the mistake.

expressions.usage: 수동태로 주어를 의도적으로 생략. "누가 초대하지 않는지"보다 "초대받지 못하는 사실" 자체를 강조
자신의 느낌을 부드럽게 표현하는 주관적 판단

patterns.pattern: You're not [past participle]. And you're never [past participle] back, curiously.
patterns.explanation: 수동태 반복으로 점층적 배제를 표현. curiously로 아이러니를 부가.
examples: You're not included. And you're never invited back, oddly.
You're not consulted. And you're never thanked, strangely.

drillQuestions.question: You're never asked _____, curiously.
That's _____ to me.

sentences.notes: "asked"와 "asked back"에서 주어(초대하는 사람)가 의도적으로 생략된 수동태. 누가 초대하지 않는지가 아니라, 교육자가 배제된다는 사실 자체가 유머의 핵심. curiously는 문장 끝에서 아이러니를 더한다.
pronunciationNotes: asked: /æskt/ — "애스크트", sk+t 자음군 주의. ed는 /t/ 발음

sentences.original: But if you are, and you say to somebody, you know, they say, "What do you do?" and you say you work in education, you can see the blood run from their face.
sentences.translation: 하지만 만약 초대를 받았는데, 누군가에게 말하게 되면—있잖아요, 그들이 "무슨 일을 하세요?"라고 물어보고, 교육 분야에서 일한다고 하면, 그들의 얼굴에서 혈기가 빠지는 것을 볼 수 있습니다.

grammarElements.text: you say you work in education
grammarElements.label: 간접인용절 (that 생략)

grammarElements.text: you can see the blood run from their face
grammarElements.label: 주절 (지각동사 see + 목적어 + 원형부정사)

vocabulary.example: I could see the blood run from her face when she heard the news.

expressions.usage: 구어에서 청중과 친밀감을 유지하는 필러(filler). 의미 없이 말의 흐름을 유지

expressions.phrase: see the blood run from their face
expressions.usage: 극적인 공포나 불편함을 시각적으로 묘사

patterns.pattern: you can see [object] [bare infinitive] from [body part]
patterns.explanation: 지각동사(see) + 목적어 + 원형부정사 구조. 5형식. 신체적 반응을 실시간으로 묘사.
examples: You can see the color drain from their face.
You can see the tension rise in their shoulders.

drillQuestions.question: You can see the blood _____ from their face.
"What do you _____?" is a common way to ask about someone's job.

sentences.notes: 지각동사 see + 목적어(the blood) + 원형부정사(run)는 5형식 구조. "the blood run from their face"는 관용적 과장표현으로, 교육에 대한 대화를 꺼리는 사람들의 반응을 코믹하게 묘사한다. "you know"는 의미 없는 삽입어.

koreanAlignment.en: you say you work in education
you can see the blood run from their face
koreanAlignment.ko: 얼굴에서 혈기가 빠지는 것을 볼 수 있습니다

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 과장법(hyperbole) — 얼굴에서 피가 빠지는 시각적 이미지로 일상적 불편함을 극적으로 과장

sentences.original: They're like, "Oh my God. Why me?" "My one night out all week."
sentences.translation: 그들은 이렇게 반응합니다. "세상에. 왜 하필 나한테?" "일주일에 딱 하루 나온 밤인데."

grammarElements.text: My one night out all week
grammarElements.label: 직접인용 (명사구 — 독립적)

vocabulary.example: He was like, 'No way!'
Let's have a night out this Friday.

expressions.usage: 구어체에서 "said" 대신 사용하는 인용 표현. 반응이나 태도까지 포함하는 뉘앙스

expressions.phrase: my one night out all week
expressions.usage: 유일한 기회가 망쳤다는 한탄. 명사구만으로 완전한 감정을 전달

patterns.pattern: They're like, "[reaction]"
patterns.explanation: "be like"로 타인의 반응을 생생하게 재현하는 비격식 인용 패턴. 성인 영어에서도 매우 자주 사용.
examples: She was like, 'Are you serious?'
They were like, 'We had no idea.'

drillQuestions.question: They're _____, "Oh my God. Why me?"
My one _____ out all week.

sentences.notes: "be like"는 1990년대부터 영어 구어에서 "said" 대신 인용할 때 사용되는 표현. 정식 문법에서는 비표준이지만, 실제 대화에서 매우 흔하다. "My one night out all week"은 동사 없이 명사구만으로 한탄을 표현하는 구어적 축약.

koreanAlignment.en: My one night out all week

pronunciationNotes: Oh my God: /oʊ maɪ ɡɑːd/ — 구어에서 "오마이갓" 또는 "오엠지(OMG)"

sentences.original: But if you ask about their education, they pin you to the wall, because it's one of those things that goes deep with people, am I right?
sentences.translation: 하지만 그들의 교육에 대해 물어보면, 그들은 당신을 벽에 붙잡아 놓습니다. 왜냐하면 교육은 사람들에게 깊이 와닿는 것들 중 하나이기 때문입니다. 그렇지 않나요?

grammarElements.text: if you ask about their education
grammarElements.label: 조건절 (if)

grammarElements.text: they pin you to the wall
grammarElements.label: 주절

grammarElements.text: because it's one of those things
grammarElements.label: 원인절 (because)

vocabulary.example: He pinned me to the wall with questions about my project.
Music goes deep with many people.

expressions.usage: 누군가가 열정적으로 대화를 이어갈 때의 과장 표현
청중의 동의를 구하는 비전형적 부가의문문. "isn't it?"보다 직접적
보편적 경험이나 현상을 일반화할 때

patterns.pattern: It's one of those things that [verb] deep with people, am I right?
patterns.explanation: 청중의 공감을 유도하는 패턴. 보편적 경험을 환기하고 동의를 요청.
examples: It's one of those things that resonates with everyone, am I right?
It's one of those topics that hits close to home, am I right?

drillQuestions.question: It's one of those things that _____ deep with people.
They pin you _____ the wall.

sentences.notes: "pin you to the wall"는 물리적 이미지(벽에 못 박다)에서 온 관용표현으로, 열정적으로 대화를 독점하는 행동을 묘사. "goes deep"에서 주어가 "one of those things"이므로 단수 동사(goes) 사용에 주의. "am I right?"는 영국 구어에서 흔한 동의 요청.

koreanAlignment.en: if you ask about their education
they pin you to the wall
because it's one of those things
koreanAlignment.ko: 왜냐하면 그것은 ~한 것들 중 하나이기 때문입니다

pronunciationNotes: am I right: /æm aɪ ˈraɪt/ — 빠르게 "애마이라잇"으로 연음
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 청중 참여(audience engagement) — "am I right?"로 직접 동의를 구하며 쌍방향 소통의 분위기 조성

sentences.original: So I have a big interest in education, and I think we all do. We have a huge vested interest in it, partly because it's education that's meant to take us into this future that we can't grasp.
sentences.translation: 그래서 저는 교육에 큰 관심이 있고, 우리 모두 그렇다고 생각합니다. 우리는 교육에 큰 기득권적 이해관계가 있습니다. 부분적으로는 우리가 이해할 수 없는 미래로 우리를 이끌어가는 것이 바로 교육이기 때문입니다.

grammarElements.text: I have a big interest in education
grammarElements.label: 주절

grammarElements.text: and I think we all do
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (do = have a big interest)

grammarElements.text: We have a huge vested interest in it
grammarElements.label: 주절

grammarElements.text: partly because it's education that's meant to take us into this future
grammarElements.label: 원인절 (강조구문 — it is ... that)

vocabulary.example: Politicians have a vested interest in education policy.
It's a difficult concept to grasp.
We're meant to be together.

expressions.usage: 누군가가 특정 결과에 개인적 이해관계가 걸려 있을 때
강조구문(cleft sentence): it is X that로 X를 강조

patterns.pattern: It's [X] that's meant to [verb] us into [this future] that we can't [verb]
patterns.explanation: 강조구문(cleft sentence) + 관계대명사의 중첩. 교육의 역할을 강조하면서 미래의 불확실성을 이중으로 표현.
examples: It's innovation that's meant to carry us into this era that we can't predict.
It's leadership that's meant to guide us through these challenges that we can't foresee.

drillQuestions.question: We have a huge _____ interest in education.
This future that we can't _____.

sentences.notes: "it's education that's meant to..."는 강조구문(cleft sentence). 일반 어순은 "education is meant to take us into this future"이지만, it is ... that 구조로 "education"을 강조. "vested interest"는 법률/금융 용어에서 온 표현으로, 개인적 이해관계가 깊이 걸려 있음을 의미.

koreanAlignment.en: I have a big interest in education
and I think we all do
We have a huge vested interest in it
koreanAlignment.ko: 우리는 교육에 큰 기득권적 이해관계가 있습니다

koreanAlignment.en: that's meant to take us into this future
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 미래로 우리를 이끌어가도록 되어 있는

pronunciationNotes: vested: /ˈvestɪd/ — "베스티드", v와 b 구별 주의. 입술을 물지 않으면 b가 됨
grasp: /ɡræsp/ — "그래스프", sp 자음군 끝 발음 주의
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 강조구문(cleft sentence) — "it's education that..."로 교육의 역할을 구조적으로 강조

sentences.original: If you think of it, children starting school this year will be retiring in 2065.
sentences.translation: 생각해보면, 올해 학교에 입학하는 아이들은 2065년에 은퇴하게 됩니다.

vocabulary.example: She plans to retire at 65.
Think of the possibilities!

expressions.usage: 청중에게 잠시 생각해보라는 초대. 새로운 관점을 제시하기 전 사용
미래진행형으로 미래의 특정 시점에 진행 중인 상황을 표현

patterns.pattern: If you think of it, [subject present participle] [time] will be [verb-ing] in [future year]
patterns.explanation: 현재의 시작점과 먼 미래의 종착점을 대비하여 시간의 스케일을 체감시키는 패턴.
examples: If you think of it, babies born today will be living in 2100.
If you think of it, students entering college now will be working until 2070.

drillQuestions.question: Children _____ school this year will be retiring in 2065.
They will be _____ in 2065.

sentences.notes: "children starting school"에서 starting은 현재분사의 후치수식. "children who are starting school"의 축약. 미래진행형(will be retiring)은 단순미래(will retire)보다 그 시점의 상황을 더 생생하게 그린다. 이 문장은 2006년 강연 기준으로 계산된 것.
pronunciationNotes: children: /ˈtʃɪldrən/ — "칠드런", l 다음 dr 연결 주의
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 시간적 대비(temporal contrast) — 현재(올해 입학)와 먼 미래(2065년 은퇴)를 병치하여 교육의 장기적 영향력을 체감시킴

sentences.original: Nobody has a clue, despite all the expertise that's been on parade for the past four days, what the world will look like in five years' time.
sentences.translation: 지난 4일간 과시된 모든 전문 지식에도 불구하고, 5년 후 세상이 어떤 모습일지 아무도 모릅니다.

grammarElements.text: despite all the expertise that's been on parade for the past four days
grammarElements.label: 양보 전치사구 (despite) + 관계대명사절

grammarElements.text: what the world will look like in five years' time
grammarElements.label: 명사절 (간접의문문 — clue의 동격)

vocabulary.example: I don't have a clue what he's talking about.
She has expertise in international law.
Their wealth was on parade for everyone to see.

expressions.usage: "nobody knows"보다 구어적이고 강한 표현
군대 퍼레이드의 이미지에서 온 관용표현. 능력이나 지식이 자랑스럽게 드러나는 상황
미래의 특정 시점을 표현. 소유격 apostrophe 주의

patterns.pattern: Nobody has a clue, despite [impressive qualifier], what [question] ...
patterns.explanation: 강력한 양보절을 삽입하여, 아무리 뛰어난 전문가도 모른다는 점을 극적으로 강조.
examples: Nobody has a clue, despite years of research, what causes this disease.
Nobody knows, despite all the data, where the market is heading.

drillQuestions.question: Nobody has a _____ what the future looks like.
_____ all the expertise, nobody knows the answer.

sentences.notes: 이 문장의 핵심은 despite 절이 주절과 종속절 사이에 삽입되어 있다는 것. 일반 어순은 "Nobody has a clue what the world will look like, despite..."이지만, despite를 중간에 삽입하여 전문성의 무용함을 강조. "five years' time"에서 apostrophe 위치에 주의.

koreanAlignment.en: that's been on parade for the past four days
what the world will look like

sentences.original: And yet, we're meant to be educating them for it. So the unpredictability, I think, is extraordinary.
sentences.translation: 그런데도 우리는 그 미래를 위해 아이들을 교육하도록 되어 있습니다. 그래서 이 예측 불가능성은, 제 생각에, 놀랍습니다.

grammarElements.text: we're meant to be educating them
grammarElements.label: 주절 (be meant to + 진행형)

vocabulary.example: The unpredictability of the weather makes farming difficult.
He was tired, and yet he kept working.
This medicine is meant to be taken with food.

expressions.usage: "but"보다 강한 역접. 앞 문장과의 모순을 극적으로 부각
be meant to + 진행형으로 "현재 진행 중인 의무"를 나타냄

patterns.pattern: And yet, we're meant to be [verb-ing] ... So the [abstract noun], I think, is extraordinary.
patterns.explanation: 역접(and yet)으로 모순을 드러낸 후, 삽입절(I think)과 함께 결론을 내리는 패턴.
examples: And yet, we're meant to be planning for it. So the uncertainty, I think, is remarkable.
And yet, we're expected to be solving it. So the complexity, I believe, is overwhelming.

drillQuestions.question: _____ yet, we're meant to be educating them for it.
The _____, I think, is extraordinary.

sentences.notes: unpredictability는 un(부정) + predict(예측하다) + ability(능력) + -ity(명사)로 구성된 파생어. 7음절의 긴 단어로 그 자체가 "복잡하고 다루기 어려운 것"이라는 느낌을 전달. "I think"가 주어와 동사 사이에 삽입된 것은 구어체 특징.

koreanAlignment.en: we're meant to be educating them

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 역설(paradox) — 미래를 모르면서 미래를 위해 교육한다는 모순을 부각하여 논점 강화

sentences.original: And the third part of this is that we've all agreed, nonetheless, on the really extraordinary capacities that children have—their capacities for innovation.
sentences.translation: 그리고 세 번째는, 그럼에도 불구하고 우리 모두가 아이들이 가진 정말 놀라운 능력—혁신 능력—에 동의했다는 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: on the really extraordinary capacities that children have
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (대상) + 관계대명사절

vocabulary.example: Children have a remarkable capacity for learning.
Innovation drives economic growth.
It was raining; nonetheless, we went for a walk.

expressions.usage: nevertheless와 동의어. however보다 격식적. 앞의 불확실성에도 불구하고 합의가 있었음을 강조
대시(—)로 앞의 "extraordinary capacities"를 구체적으로 부연

patterns.pattern: We've all agreed, nonetheless, on the [adjective] [noun] that [subject] have—their [noun] for [noun]
patterns.explanation: 합의 사항을 제시하고, 대시 뒤에 핵심을 구체화하는 패턴. 3부 구조의 마지막 주제 도입.
examples: We've all agreed on the remarkable abilities that humans have—their ability for adaptation.
We've all recognized the unique qualities that artists have—their sensitivity to the world.

drillQuestions.question: We've all agreed, _____, on the extraordinary capacities.
Children have extraordinary _____ for innovation.

sentences.notes: "the third part of this"로 앞서 예고한 3가지 주제의 마지막을 도입. 대시(—) 뒤의 "their capacities for innovation"은 동격으로 앞의 "extraordinary capacities"를 구체화. nonetheless가 주어와 동사 사이에 삽입되어 양보의 의미를 더함.

koreanAlignment.en: And the third part of this is

sentences.original: And she's exceptional, but I think she's not, so to speak, exceptional in the whole of childhood.
sentences.translation: 그녀는 예외적이지만, 말하자면, 어린 시절 전체를 놓고 보면 예외적이지 않다고 생각합니다.

vocabulary.example: She is an exceptional musician.
He's the captain of the team, so to speak.

expressions.usage: 비유적이거나 부정확할 수 있는 표현을 완화할 때 사용하는 삽입어
범위를 확대하여 일반화할 때

patterns.pattern: She's [adjective], but ... she's not, so to speak, [adjective] in the whole of [noun]
patterns.explanation: 같은 형용사를 두 번 사용하되 범위를 바꿔 역설을 만드는 패턴. "개인으로는 특별하지만 집단으로는 아니다."
examples: He's unique, but he's not, so to speak, unique in the history of art.
This case is rare, but it's not, so to speak, rare in the broader population.

drillQuestions.question: She's exceptional, but she's not, _____ to speak, exceptional in childhood.
She's not exceptional in the _____ of childhood.

sentences.notes: 같은 단어 "exceptional"을 두 번 사용하되 의미를 뒤집는 고급 수사법. 개인적 수준에서는 예외적이지만, 아이들 전체로 보면 모든 아이가 그런 잠재력을 가지고 있다는 뜻. "so to speak"는 "정확한 표현은 아닐 수 있지만"이라는 완화 장치.

koreanAlignment.en: exceptional in the whole of childhood

pronunciationNotes: so to speak: /soʊ tə spiːk/ — "소우 터 스피크", 빠르게 연음됨
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 역설(paradox) — 같은 단어를 반복하면서 의미를 뒤집어 논점을 강화. "예외적이면서 예외적이지 않다"

sentences.original: What you have there is a person of extraordinary dedication who found a talent.
sentences.translation: 거기서 볼 수 있는 것은 재능을 발견한 놀라운 헌신의 사람입니다.

vocabulary.example: Her dedication to the project was admirable.
She has a natural talent for music.

expressions.usage: 의사 분열문으로 대상을 재정의하는 구어적 강조 표현
"of + 추상명사"로 성격이나 특질을 표현하는 격식적 구조

patterns.pattern: What you have there is [noun phrase] who [verb past] [noun]
patterns.explanation: 의사 분열문(pseudo-cleft)으로 핵심을 재정의하는 패턴. 앞서 본 것을 새로운 관점에서 해석.
examples: What you have there is a leader who inspired millions.
What you see here is a system that actually works.

drillQuestions.question: _____ you have there is a person of extraordinary dedication.
A person of extraordinary _____ who found a talent.

sentences.notes: "What you have there is..."는 의사 분열문(pseudo-cleft sentence)의 구어적 변형. "a person of extraordinary dedication"에서 "of + 추상명사"는 형용사를 대신하는 격식적 표현(= an extraordinarily dedicated person).

koreanAlignment.en: is a person of extraordinary dedication

sentences.original: And my contention is, all kids have tremendous talents, and we squander them, pretty ruthlessly.
sentences.translation: 그리고 제 주장은, 모든 아이들이 엄청난 재능을 가지고 있으며, 우리는 그것을 꽤 무자비하게 낭비한다는 것입니다.

vocabulary.example: His contention is that the system needs reform.
The team made tremendous progress this year.
Don't squander this opportunity.
The company ruthlessly cut costs.

expressions.usage: 공식적으로 자신의 핵심 논지를 선언할 때. thesis statement의 구어적 표현
pretty(꽤)가 ruthlessly(무자비하게)를 완화하면서도 강조. 영국 영어에서 pretty는 상당히(fairly) 의미

patterns.pattern: My contention is, [thesis statement], and we [verb] them, [adverb].
patterns.explanation: 강연의 핵심 주장(thesis)을 선언하는 패턴. 주장 후 "and we..."로 현실의 문제를 대비.
examples: My contention is, all students can succeed, and we fail them, systematically.
My argument is, creativity is essential, and we suppress it, relentlessly.

drillQuestions.question: My _____ is, all kids have tremendous talents.
We _____ them, pretty ruthlessly.

sentences.notes: squander는 "낭비하다"라는 뜻의 고급 어휘로, waste보다 의도적이고 안타까운 낭비를 함의. "pretty ruthlessly"에서 pretty는 구어적 완화장치이지만, 오히려 ruthlessly의 충격을 효과적으로 전달한다. 이 문장은 강연의 핵심 thesis statement.

koreanAlignment.ko: 모든 아이들이 엄청난 재능을 가지고 있다는 것입니다

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 논제 선언(thesis statement) — 강연의 핵심 주장을 명시적으로 선언하여 이후 논증의 방향을 설정

sentences.original: So I want to talk about education, and I want to talk about creativity.
sentences.translation: 그래서 저는 교육에 대해, 그리고 창의성에 대해 이야기하고 싶습니다.

grammarElements.text: I want to talk about education
grammarElements.label: 주절

grammarElements.text: and I want to talk about creativity
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (병렬 반복)

vocabulary.example: Let's talk about the project.

expressions.phrase: I want to talk about X, and I want to talk about Y
expressions.meaning: X에 대해, 그리고 Y에 대해 이야기하고 싶다
expressions.usage: 의도적 반복으로 두 주제를 동등한 비중으로 제시. 축약하지 않고 반복하여 강조.

patterns.pattern: I want to talk about [X], and I want to talk about [Y].
patterns.explanation: 병렬 반복(parallelism)으로 두 핵심 주제를 동등하게 제시하는 패턴. 축약 가능하지만 의도적으로 반복.
examples: I want to talk about justice, and I want to talk about equality.
I want to discuss the problem, and I want to discuss the solution.

drillQuestions.question: I want to talk _____ education.
I want to talk about education, _____ I want to talk about creativity.

sentences.notes: 문법적으로는 단순하지만, "I want to talk about"을 의도적으로 두 번 반복하는 것이 핵심. "I want to talk about education and creativity"로 축약할 수 있지만, 반복함으로써 두 주제를 각각 독립적으로 강조하는 수사적 효과.

koreanAlignment.en: So I want to talk about education
koreanAlignment.ko: 그래서 저는 교육에 대해 이야기하고 싶습니다

koreanAlignment.en: and I want to talk about creativity

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 병렬 반복(anaphora) — "I want to talk about"을 반복하여 두 주제의 동등한 중요성을 강조

sentences.original: My contention is that creativity now is as important in education as literacy, and we should treat it with the same status.
sentences.translation: 제 주장은, 창의성이 이제 교육에서 문해력만큼이나 중요하며, 우리가 같은 지위로 대우해야 한다는 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: that creativity now is as important in education as literacy
grammarElements.label: that절 (보어 — as...as 동등비교)

grammarElements.text: and we should treat it with the same status
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (당위)

vocabulary.example: Improving literacy rates is a national priority.
Education should be given the highest status.
We should treat all students equally.

expressions.usage: as...as 동등비교 구문으로 두 대상의 동등한 가치를 주장

expressions.phrase: treat it with the same status
expressions.usage: 제도적, 정책적 차원의 동등한 대우를 요구할 때

patterns.pattern: My contention is that [X] is as [adjective] as [Y], and we should [verb] it with the same [noun].
patterns.explanation: 동등비교(as...as) + 당위(should)를 결합하여 핵심 주장을 마무리하는 패턴. 강연의 thesis를 최종 정리.
examples: My contention is that arts are as valuable as science, and we should fund them with the same priority.
My argument is that emotional intelligence is as crucial as IQ, and we should nurture it with the same effort.

drillQuestions.question: Creativity is as important _____ education as literacy.
Creativity is _____ important as literacy.
We should treat it with the _____ status.

sentences.notes: 이 문장은 강연 전체의 핵심 thesis. as...as 동등비교를 사용하여 창의성과 문해력을 같은 수준에 놓는다. "My contention is"가 두 번째로 등장하여 논지를 강화. literacy는 현대 교육에서 가장 기본적인 능력으로 간주되므로, 창의성을 그와 동등하게 놓는 것은 매우 강력한 주장.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 동등비교 논증(argument by equivalence) — as...as 구문으로 창의성과 문해력을 동등하게 놓아 기존 교육의 우선순위에 도전
